title: Reading Passages with Questions
instructions: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions. Answers must be found only within the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Ausable was a secret agent who lived in Paris. He was very fat and moved slowly, unlike the spies shown in films. Fowler, a writer, felt disappointed when he first met Ausable because he expected excitement and danger.

Fowler had imagined secret agents as slim, smart, and adventurous. Ausable, however, spoke calmly and showed no excitement. Fowler felt that meeting Ausable would not give him any thrilling experience.
questions: How did Fowler imagine secret agents?
How did Ausable behave while speaking?
What kind of experience did Fowler expect?
Why did Fowler feel let down?

passages.paragraph: Suddenly, a man named Max entered Ausable’s room with a gun in his hand. He was another secret agent and looked threatening. His sudden entry created fear and tension in the room.
questions: What feeling did his entry create?

passages.paragraph: Max had come to steal an important report from Ausable. The report was about new missiles. Max believed that threatening Ausable with a gun would help him get the report easily.
questions: Why did Max come to Ausable’s room?
What was Max trying to steal?
How did Max plan to get the report?
What weapon did Max use to threaten Ausable?

passages.paragraph: Ausable remained calm even when Max pointed a gun at him. He told Max that the room had a balcony through which enemies often entered. His calm tone made the story sound believable.
questions: How did Ausable react to the threat?
What did Ausable say about the room?
Through what way did Ausable claim enemies entered?
Why did the story sound believable?

passages.paragraph: In reality, there was no balcony outside the room. Ausable had invented the story to confuse Max. Max believed the story and became nervous.
questions: How did Max feel after hearing it?

passages.paragraph: A knock was heard at the door, and Max became frightened. Ausable said it must be the police. Hearing this, Max panicked and looked for a way to escape.
questions: How did Max react to the knock?
Who did Ausable say was at the door?
What did Max try to do next?

passages.paragraph: Believing the balcony existed, Max rushed to escape through it. He opened the window and jumped out. Since there was no balcony, Max fell to his death.
questions: Why did Max rush to escape?
Through where did Max try to escape?

passages.paragraph: The knock on the door was actually made by a waiter named Henry. He had come to deliver drinks ordered earlier. His arrival unknowingly caused Max’s downfall.
questions: How did his arrival affect Max?

passages.paragraph: Fowler was thrilled by the incident. He finally experienced real danger and excitement. He understood that intelligence and presence of mind were more important than physical strength.
questions: How did Fowler feel after the incident?
What quality proved more important than strength?
